message:
test: pin sched.h conformance via differential harness

Adds tests/conformance_diff_sched.rs covering 7 functions:
- sched_yield: return value match
- sched_get_priority_min/max: 5 policies (OTHER/FIFO/RR/BATCH/IDLE)
  + invalid-policy edge
- sched_getscheduler: self + INT_MAX-pid edge
- sched_getparam: self priority match
- sched_getaffinity: 128B mask byte-exact match against glibc

Setters intentionally excluded (CAP_SYS_NICE required, would mutate
process state).

8 tests pass, 0 divergences against glibc.

Bead: bd-sch1

files.patch:
@@ -686,8 +686,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-el0v8","title":"abi tests: synchronous_gai_wrappers flakes under --test-threads=16 (fmemopen/tmpfile fixed)","description":"## Symptom

Three additional unit tests flake intermittently under high
parallelism (--test-threads=16, ~25% combined hit rate across 12
sweep iterations), distinct from bd-7v4oe (closed):

| Test | Hit rate (12 sweeps) | File |
|------|---------------------|------|
| `synchronous_gai_wrappers_match_host_degenerate_contracts` | 3/12 (25%) | DNS/network |
| `tmpfile_closes_without_leaking_named_tmp_entries` | 1/12 (~8%) | filesystem |
| `fmemopen_write_creates_stream` | 1/12 (~8%) | stdio |

The bd-7v4oe fix (registry RAII guard) verified its own target
flake is gone in this same sweep — these are separate races.

## Likely categories

1. **synchronous_gai_wrappers** — touches `getaddrinfo` and friends
   which read /etc/resolv.conf, /etc/hosts, etc. Under parallelism
   the host glibc resolver may serialize on shared file descriptors
   or DNS cache state, producing different EAI_* codes than
   "degenerate" baseline. Fix candidate: serialize the test (or
   subset) with a shared lock.
2. **tmpfile_closes_without_leaking_named_tmp_entries** — POSIX
   `tmpfile()` creates an unlinked file in /tmp. Under parallel
   pressure the directory enumeration check may see entries from
   other in-flight `tmpfile()` calls. Fix candidate: use a unique
   subdirectory or accept transient races in the leak count check.
3. **fmemopen_write_creates_stream** — fmemopen builds a memory-
   backed FILE*. Could share registry state with other stream
   tests. Fix candidate: same RAII registry-cleanup pattern as
   bd-7v4oe.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3) — test-only flakes that don't affect production
behavior. Worth fixing because they pollute CI and mask real
regressions in stress sweeps. The bd-7v4oe pattern (RAII cleanup
guards) likely applies to (3); (1) and (2) need targeted analysis
of the host syscall surface they exercise.","notes":"Partial fix: fmemopen flake closed in dd82f863 (STREAM_REGISTRY_PROBE_LOCK), tmpfile flake closed in 18baf908 (drop racy redundant /tmp enumeration). synchronous_gai_wrappers flake remains open — needs cross-test DNS state serialization across multiple test binaries (host glibc gai async queue shares state across all in-process callers). Reproduces only when running --tests (multiple binaries), not within unistd_abi_test alone. 10/10 isolated runs pass.

Fully fixed: three-part fix in this cycle.
1. GAI_TEST_LOCK (31f5b9f2): intra-binary serialization across 16 gai_*/getaddrinfo_a tests. Single-pass parity now stable.
2. GaicbShape struct size (9d4bd2f5): test's GaicbShape was 32 bytes but glibc's struct gaicb is 56 (adds __return + __unused[5]). host_gai_error reads __return — with the short struct it read uninitialized stack past the boundary, producing divergent host-vs-abi results under parallel pressure. Real ABI-correctness bug surfaced by the flake.
3. xdr_reference calloc (fd7c7cba): bonus fix. Stress uncovered a SIGABRT / misaligned-pointer deref in xdr_reference_depth_limit regression test; root cause was libc::malloc leaving inner pointer fields uninitialized for recursive procs. Switched to libc::calloc (mirrors glibc xdr_reference's malloc+memset idiom).
Verified: 5/5 xdr_abi_test --test-threads=16 iters pass; earlier gai 7/8 iters had zero gai failures (xdr SIGSEGV was the #3 bug, now also fixed). Full abi 8x stress in flight.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:43:15.145493962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:58:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T02:58:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-emwy","title":"syscall: futex wait-bitset veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T15:09:55.617591752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:13:44.464779692Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T15:13:44.462848217Z","close_reason":"Implemented futex wait-bitset veneer coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-epeg","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)","description":"Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:16.769752763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645348954Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645135059Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","cross-crate","franken-kernel","frankenlibc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1293,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started a bounded `bd-epeg` slice by extending the local fallback ID/schema contract into `alien_cs_metrics`.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/alien_cs_metrics.rs` to import the local fallback `DecisionId` and `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- Metric event JSONL rows now emit `decision_id` and `schema_version`.
- Aggregate snapshot JSONL rows now emit `decision_id: 0` and `schema_version`.
- Tightened the Alien CS JSONL tests to require those fields and to assert deterministic values.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane alien_cs_metrics -- --nocapture`
  Expected: alien_cs_metrics targeted tests pass with the updated JSONL contract.
  Result: passed, 14 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- `bv --robot-triage/--robot-next/--robot-insights` still ranks the TSM epic as the strongest actionable lane. This bead was the next concrete, useful step because it extends canonical fallback IDs into another structured evidence exporter without waiting for full `franken_kernel` integration.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project currently shows only `MistyGrove` registered, so there were no peer agents to notify directly.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:31Z"},{"id":1294,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a second `bd-epeg` slice by aligning the HJI proof artifact schema tag with the shared fallback membrane schema.

- Tightened `viability_artifact_reports_expected_kernel_counts` to assert `schema_version == "1.0"`.

Verification:

  Result: passed.

  Expected: HJI artifact test passes with shared schema version.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

  Expected: witness proof remains valid after schema change.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- This continues the same actionable `bd-epeg` lane chosen from `bv --robot-*`: move remaining structured/proof artifacts onto the shared fallback schema instead of isolated local version strings.
- Agent Mail remained empty and no other agents are registered in this project, so there were no direct coordination messages to send or answer.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:46:51Z"},{"id":1295,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting another bounded bd-epeg slice: aligning runtime_math/evidence JSON export with the shared fallback ID/schema contract. Reserving `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` now and will verify with targeted rch cargo check/test once patched.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:11:34Z"},{"id":1296,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented another bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

The exporter already emits canonical fallback evidence envelopes for reverse-round, calibration, and snapshot rows, but the current regression still leans on raw `jsonl.contains(...)` checks; replacing that with parsed JSON assertions now.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:04:18Z"},{"id":1314,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Tightened two more runtime_math JSONL regressions in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs. `reverse_round_diversity_snapshot_matches_jsonl_contract_rows` now parses the emitted rows and requires canonical `runtime_math::reverse_round::{selection,coverage,diversity}::...` trace scopes plus schema/bead/scenario/api/symbol/decision-path fields instead of relying on raw string containment. `runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields` now also parses and validates the dispatch/evidence/calibration/reverse-round rows, enforcing canonical trace scopes and schema envelope for those rows too. Verified with `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`, `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane reverse_round_diversity_snapshot_matches_jsonl_contract_rows -- --nocapture`, and `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture`.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:06:19Z"},{"id":1315,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran `bv --format json --robot-triage`, `--robot-next`, and `--robot-insights`; top graph picks are still `bd-2vv` and `bd-32e`, so I am continuing the actionable `bd-epeg` runtime-math evidence lane. Starting the next bounded slice now by tightening another weak exporter regression in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:27:16Z"},{"id":1316,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Tightened `runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs` in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs. -{"id":"bd-errh2","title":"FIX: err.h progname source — use argv[0] basename, not /proc/self/comm (DISC-ERR-001)","description":"Discovered by err.h diff harness. fl::err_abi::get_progname() reads /proc/self/comm which the kernel truncates to TASK_COMM_LEN=15 chars and which is per-task (forked children inherit, then any prctl(PR_SET_NAME) shifts it). glibc reads __progname which is the basename of argv[0] — full executable name. Result: fl warns/errs print 'conformance_dif: ...' instead of 'conformance_diff_err_h-30ba6209...: ...'.  {"id":"bd-ily44","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] iconv() TOCTOU use-after-free with concurrent iconv_close()","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/iconv_abi.rs lines 176-188, the iconv() function validates the descriptor handle via is_known_handle(cd) (HashSet lookup), then dereferences via 'unsafe { &mut *cd.cast::<IconvDescriptor>() }'. There is no synchronization between the lookup and the dereference.

If thread B calls iconv_close(cd) between thread A's is_known_handle check and the &mut * dereference, the descriptor's Box is freed (line 350: 'unsafe { Box::from_raw(cd.cast::<IconvDescriptor>()) }'), and thread A then dereferences freed memory --- use-after-free.

POSIX does not explicitly forbid concurrent iconv()/iconv_close() on the same descriptor, but a robust libc should not crash with UAF. Mitigation options:

1. Hold the handles() Mutex for the duration of iconv() (cheap, but serializes all conversions across descriptors).
2. Replace the HashSet<usize> registry with HashMap<usize, Arc<Mutex<IconvDescriptor>>> so iconv() takes an Arc clone before releasing the registry lock; iconv_close removes the registry entry and drops one Arc reference, but the active iconv() call's Arc keeps the descriptor alive until it returns.
3. Use a generational handle scheme (sequence counter + slot index) to detect stale handles.

Option 2 is recommended: per-descriptor mutex avoids serialization across descriptors, and Arc reference-counting cleanly handles the lifetime issue.

Severity: HIGH — UAF in interposed libc function under multi-threaded use.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:52:59.789930171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:30:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: commit c8003957 (peer agent) refactored iconv registry to Arc<Mutex<IconvDescriptor>>; concurrent close + iconv no longer UAFs. 41/41 iconv tests pass including the new TOCTOU regression.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-imo7","title":"Metamorphic tests for raw syscall signal semantics","description":"The syscall veneer tests exercise signalfd4 and timer sigevent but lack direct MR coverage for sys_kill/sys_tgkill/sys_tkill/sys_rt_sigaction/sys_rt_sigprocmask roundtrip semantics. Add: (1) kill(self, 0) succeeds (POSIX null signal permission check), (2) kill(self, invalid_sig) fails with EINVAL, (3) tgkill(pid, tid, 0) ≡ tkill(tid, 0) parity for self-thread, (4) rt_sigprocmask get-set-restore is invertive. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs. One MR per commit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:53:42.606540761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.761683183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.759781481Z","close_reason":"Added 4 metamorphic raw-syscall signal tests across 3 commits: 58cfdf4 (kill self null-signal + out-of-range rejection), f7f1055 (tgkill/tkill parity + tgid mismatch ESRCH), 01975f0 (rt_sigprocmask invertive get/set/restore). Fills veneer coverage gap — previously only signalfd4/timer_create_sigevent tested signal-related syscalls.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-j19j6","title":"aarch64 raw syscall + clone-tail-args code paths have no CI cross-compile check","description":"## Defect

frankenlibc-core/src/syscall/raw.rs declares 10 aarch64 syscall0..
syscall6 blocks via #[cfg(target_arch = \"aarch64\")] and a separate
current_clone_tail_args variant for aarch64's different clone
argument order. frankenlibc-core/src/lib.rs and frankenlibc-abi/
src/lib.rs both gate compilation on cfg(any(x86_64, aarch64)), and
multiple closed beads (bd-10pq, bd-h5x) point to aarch64 as
'supported' / 'active bring-up'.

Reality check: there is no CI job, no cross-compile test, and no
deterministic gate script that actually compiles ANY crate for
aarch64. `rg --files | xargs -I _ rg 'aarch64'` only hits the
source-level cfg attrs — no workflow, no cross-build in scripts/,
no triple override in Cargo metadata.

If any of the 10 aarch64 syscall blocks has a typo or wrong
register constraint, we'd only notice when someone first tries
to build on an actual aarch64 host. Meanwhile, the bead closures
overstate current support.

## Fix

Add one of:
1. `scripts/check_aarch64_crosscompile.sh` that runs `cargo check
   --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu -p frankenlibc-core -p
   frankenlibc-abi` with the cross toolchain, as a deterministic
   gate that can run in CI.
2. A tests/conformance/ fixture that exercises clone-tail-args
   layout via a type-level compile check.

Option 1 is the minimal-scope fix — cross-compile check catches
register-constraint typos and missing cfg gates without needing
an aarch64 runner.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P2). No active exploitation — x86_64 is the primary
target and all known consumers use it. But the closure notes
on bd-10pq and bd-h5x claim aarch64 support without CI evidence,
so the first aarch64 consumer will hit avoidable breakage.","notes":"Fixed in prior commit: scripts/check_aarch64_crosscompile.sh gates cargo check --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu for frankenlibc-core + frankenlibc-abi. Runs on x86_64 hosts via rustup target add (no aarch64 linker or hardware required for cargo check front-end).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T16:56:50.661732236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:15:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
